It will usually be\n// accompanied in the graph by a NodeApplyableResource representing its\n// containing resource, and should depend on that node to ensure that the\n// state is properly prepared to receive changes to instances.\ntype NodeApplyableResourceInstance struct {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n\n\tgraphNodeDeposer // implementation of GraphNodeDeposerConfig\n\n\t// forceReplace are resource instance addresses where the user wants to\n\t// force generating a replace action. This set isn't pre-filtered, so\n\t// it might contain addresses that have nothing to do with the resource\n\t// that this node represents, which the node itself must therefore ignore.\n\tforceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource     = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance   = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeCreator            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeReferencer         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeDeposer            = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable         = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachDependencies = (*NodeApplyableResourceInstance)(nil)\n)\n\n// GraphNodeCreator\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) CreateAddr() *addrs.AbsResourceInstance {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\treturn &addr\n}\n\n// GraphNodeReferencer, overriding NodeAbstractResourceInstance\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) References() []*addrs.Reference {\n\t// Start with the usual resource instance implementation\n\tret := n.NodeAbstractResourceInstance.References()\n\n\t// Applying a resource must also depend on the destruction of any of its\n\t// dependencies, since this may for example affect the outcome of\n\t// evaluating an entire list of resources with \"count\" set (by reducing\n\t// the count).\n\t//\n\t// However, we can't do this in create_before_destroy mode because that\n\t// would create a dependency cycle. We make a compromise here of requiring\n\t// changes to be updated across two applies in this case, since the first\n\t// plan will use the old values.\n\tif !n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tfor _, ref := range ret {\n\t\t\tswitch tr := ref.Subject.(type) {\n\t\t\tcase addrs.ResourceInstance:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\tcase addrs.Resource:\n\t\t\t\tnewRef := *ref // shallow copy so we can mutate\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Subject = tr.Phase(addrs.ResourceInstancePhaseDestroy)\n\t\t\t\tnewRef.Remaining = nil // can't access attributes of something being destroyed\n\t\t\t\tret = append(ret, &newRef)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn ret\n}\n\n// GraphNodeAttachDependencies\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) AttachDependencies(deps []addrs.ConfigResource) {\n\tn.Dependencies = deps\n}\n\n// GraphNodeExecutable\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\t// If there is no config, and there is no change, then we have nothing\n\t\t// to do and the change was left in the plan for informational\n\t\t// purposes only.\n\t\tchanges := ctx.Changes()\n\t\tcsrc := changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), addrs.NotDeposed)\n\t\tif csrc == nil || csrc.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] NodeApplyableResourceInstance: No config or planned change recorded for %s\", n.Addr)\n\t\t\treturn nil\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource node has no configuration attached\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"The graph node for %s has no configuration attached to it. This suggests a bug in Terraform's apply graph builder; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n\tswitch n.Config.Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\t_, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\n\t\taddr:           n.Addr,\n\t\tconfig:         n.Config,\n\t\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t})\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tchange, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\t// Stop early if we don't actually have a diff\n\tif change == nil {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif change.Action != plans.Read && change.Action != plans.NoOp {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", change.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\t// In this particular call to applyDataSource we include our planned\n\t// change, which signals that we expect this read to complete fully\n\t// with no unknown values; it'll produce an error if not.\n\tstate, repeatData, applyDiags := n.applyDataSource(ctx, change)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// If n.applyDataSource returned a nil state object with no accompanying\n\t\t// errors then it determined that the given change doesn't require\n\t\t// actually reading the data (e.g. because it was already read during\n\t\t// the plan phase) and so we're only running through here to get the\n\t\t// extra details like precondition/postcondition checks.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\"))\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\n\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(),\n\t\trepeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\t// Declare a bunch of variables that are used for state during\n\t// evaluation. Most of this are written to by-address below.\n\tvar state *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\tvar createBeforeDestroyEnabled bool\n\tvar deposedKey states.DeposedKey\n\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff for apply\n\tdiffApply, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// We don't want to do any destroys\n\t// (these are handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance instead)\n\tif diffApply == nil || diffApply.Action == plans.Delete {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.Read {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"nonsensical planned action %#v for %s; this is a bug in Terraform\", diffApply.Action, n.Addr))\n\t}\n\n\tdestroy := (diffApply.Action == plans.Delete || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\t// Get the stored action for CBD if we have a plan already\n\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = diffApply.Change.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete\n\n\tif destroy && n.CreateBeforeDestroy() {\n\t\tcreateBeforeDestroyEnabled = true\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled {\n\t\tstate := ctx.State()\n\t\tif n.PreallocatedDeposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = state.DeposeResourceInstanceObject(n.Addr)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tdeposedKey = n.PreallocatedDeposedKey\n\t\t\tstate.DeposeResourceInstanceObjectForceKey(n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t}\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: prior object for %s now deposed with key %s\", n.Addr, deposedKey)\n\t}\n\n\tstate, readDiags := n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr())\n\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Get the saved diff\n\tdiff, err := n.readDiff(ctx, providerSchema)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Make a new diff, in case we've learned new values in the state\n\t// during apply which we can now incorporate.\n\tdiffApply, _, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(ctx, diff, state, false, n.forceReplace)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Resource deferred during apply, but not during plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where a provider would mark the resource %q as deferred during apply, but not during plan. This is most likely a bug in the provider. Please file an issue with the provider.\", n.Addr,\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Compare the diffs\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPlannedChange(ctx, diff, diffApply, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiffApply = reducePlan(addr, diffApply, false)\n\t// reducePlan may have simplified our planned change\n\t// into a NoOp if it only requires destroying, since destroying\n\t// is handled by NodeDestroyResourceInstance. If so, we'll\n\t// still run through most of the logic here because we do still\n\t// need to deal with other book-keeping such as marking the\n\t// change as \"complete\", and running the author's postconditions.\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.preApplyHook(ctx, diffApply))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// If there is no change, there was nothing to apply, and we don't need to\n\t// re-write the state, but we do need to re-evaluate postconditions.\n\tif diffApply.Action == plans.NoOp {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n\t}\n\n\tstate, applyDiags := n.apply(ctx, state, diffApply, n.Config, repeatData, n.CreateBeforeDestroy())\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyDiags)\n\n\t// We clear the change out here so that future nodes don't see a change\n\t// that is already complete.\n\terr = n.writeChange(ctx, nil, \"\")\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\tif state != nil {\n\t\t// dependencies are always updated to match the configuration during apply\n\t\tstate.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\n\t}\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\t// Run Provisioners\n\tcreateNew := (diffApply.Action == plans.Create || diffApply.Action.IsReplace())\n\tapplyProvisionersDiags := n.evalApplyProvisioners(ctx, state, createNew, configs.ProvisionerWhenCreate)\n\t// the provisioner errors count as port of the apply error, so we can bundle the diags\n\tdiags = diags.Append(applyProvisionersDiags)\n\n\tstate = maybeTainted(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), state, diffApply, diags.Err())\n\n\terr = n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn diags.Append(err)\n\t}\n\n\tif createBeforeDestroyEnabled && diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\tif deposedKey == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t// This should never happen, and so it always indicates a bug.\n\t\t\t// We should evaluate this node only if we've previously deposed\n\t\t\t// an object as part of the same operation.\n\t\t\tif diffApply != nil {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This occurred during a %s action. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\taddr, diffApply.Action,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Attempt to restore non-existent deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Terraform has encountered a bug where it would need to restore a deposed object for %s without knowing a deposed object key for that object. This is a bug in Terraform; please report it!\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\taddr,\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\trestored := ctx.State().MaybeRestoreResourceInstanceDeposed(addr.Absolute(ctx.Path()), deposedKey)\n\t\t\tif restored {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s was restored as the current object\", addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] managedResourceExecute: %s deposed object %s remains deposed\", addr, deposedKey)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(n.postApplyHook(ctx, state, diags.Err()))\n\tdiags = diags.Append(updateStateHook(ctx))\n\n\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t// _after_ writing the state because we want to check against\n\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\treturn diags.Append(n.managedResourcePostconditions(ctx, repeatData))\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) managedResourcePostconditions(ctx EvalContext, repeatData instances.RepetitionData) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\n\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\n\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t)\n\treturn diags.Append(checkDiags)\n}\n\n// checkPlannedChange produces errors if the _actual_ expected value is not\n// compatible with what was recorded in the plan.\n//\n// Errors here are most often indicative of a bug in the provider, so our error\n// messages will report with that in mind. It's also possible that there's a bug\n// in Terraform's Core's own \"proposed new value\" code in EvalDiff.\nfunc (n *NodeApplyableResourceInstance) checkPlannedChange(ctx EvalContext, plannedChange, actualChange *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(addr.ContainingResource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support %q\", addr.Resource.Type))\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] checkPlannedChange: Verifying that actual change (action %s) matches planned change (action %s)\", actualChange.Action, plannedChange.Action)\n\n\tif plannedChange.Action != actualChange.Action {\n\t\tswitch {\n\t\tcase plannedChange.Action == plans.Update && actualChange.Action == plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// It's okay for an update to become a NoOp once we've filled in\n\t\t\t// all of the unknown values, since the final values might actually\n\t\t\t// match what was there before after all.\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] After incorporating new values learned so far during apply, %s change has become NoOp\", absAddr)\n\n\t\tcase (plannedChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete && actualChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate) ||\n\t\t\t(plannedChange.Action == plans.DeleteThenCreate && actualChange.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete):\n\t\t\t// If the order of replacement changed, then that is a bug in terraform\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Terraform produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, the planned action changed from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, plannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q changed the planned action from %s to %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(),\n\t\t\t\t\tplannedChange.Action, actualChange.Action,\n\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\terrs := objchange.AssertObjectCompatible(schema, plannedChange.After, actualChange.After)\n\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced inconsistent final plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"When expanding the plan for %s to include new values learned so far during apply, provider %q produced an invalid new value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tabsAddr, n.ResolvedProvider.Provider.String(), tfdiags.FormatError(err),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// maybeTainted takes the resource addr, new value, planned change, and possible\n// error from an apply operation and return a new instance object marked as\n// tainted if it appears that a create operation has failed.\nfunc maybeTainted(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, state *states.ResourceInstanceObject, change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange, err error) *states.ResourceInstanceObject {\n\tif state == nil || change == nil || err == nil {\n\t\treturn state\n\t}\n\tif state.Status == states.ObjectTainted GraphNodeConfigResource       = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeResourceInstance     = (*NodePlannableResourceInstance)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeAttachResourceConfig = GraphNodeEvalable\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\t// Eval info is different depending on what kind of resource this is\n\tswitch addr.Resource.Resource.Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\t\treturn n.ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.Config.Mode))\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tconfig := n.Config\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tvar change *plans.ResourceInstanceChange\n\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\n\t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n\t}\n\n\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n\tchange, state, deferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.planDataSource(ctx, checkRuleSeverity, n.skipPlanChanges, deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(addr, n.Dependencies))\n\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\t// A nil change here indicates that Terraform is deciding NOT to make a\n\t// change at all. In which case even if we wanted to try and defer it\n\t// (because of a dependency) we can't as there is no change to defer.\n\t//\n\t// The most common case for this is when the data source is being refreshed\n\t// but depends on unknown values or dependencies which means we just skip\n\t// refreshing the data source. We maintain that behaviour here.\n\tif change != nil && deferred != nil {\n\t\t// Then this data source got deferred by the provider during planning.\n\t\tdeferrals.ReportDataSourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Not deferred; business as usual.\n\n\t\t// write the data source into both the refresh state and the\n\t\t// working state\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, refreshState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, state, workingState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n\n\t\t// Post-conditions might block further progress. We intentionally do this\n\t\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n\t\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n\t\t)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) ephemeralResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n\t// For deferred ephemeral resources, we don't need to do anything here.\n\tif deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\n\t\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq)\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tdeferred, diags := ephemeralResourceOpen(ctx, ephemeralResourceInput{\n\t\taddr:           n.Addr,\n\t\tconfig:         n.Config,\n\t\tproviderConfig: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t})\n\n\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\t// Then this ephemeral resource has been deferred while opening.\n\t\tdeferrals.ReportEphemeralResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, deferred.Reason)\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tconfig := n.Config\n\taddr := n.ResourceInstanceAddr()\n\n\tvar instanceRefreshState *states.ResourceInstanceObject\n\n\tcheckRuleSeverity := tfdiags.Error\n\tif n.skipPlanChanges || n.preDestroyRefresh {\n\t\tcheckRuleSeverity = tfdiags.Warning\n\t}\n\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.ResolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tif config != nil {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(addr.Resource, config.Config, providerSchema))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\timporting := n.importTarget != cty.NilVal && !n.preDestroyRefresh\n\n\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n\n\t// If the resource is to be imported, we now ask the provider for an Import\n\t// and a Refresh, and save the resulting state to instanceRefreshState.\n\n\tif importing {\n\t\tif n.importTarget.IsKnown() {\n\t\t\tvar importDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState, deferred, importDiags = n.importState(ctx, addr, n.importTarget.AsString(), provider, providerSchema)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(importDiags)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Otherwise, just mark the resource as deferred without trying to\n\t\t\t// import it.\n\t\t\tdeferred = &providers.Deferred{\n\t\t\t\tReason: providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif n.Config == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Then we're supposed to be generating configuration for this\n\t\t\t\t// resource, but we can't because the configuration is unknown.\n\t\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t\t// Normally, the rest of this function would just be about\n\t\t\t\t// planning the known configuration to make sure everything we\n\t\t\t\t// do know about it is correct, but we can't even do that here.\n\t\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t\t// What we'll do is write out the address as being deferred with\n\t\t\t\t// an entirely unknown value. Then we'll skip the rest of this\n\t\t\t\t// function. (a) We're going to panic later when it complains\n\t\t\t\t// about having no configuration, and (b) the rest of the\n\t\t\t\t// function isn't doing anything as there is no configuration\n\t\t\t\t// to validate.\n\n\t\t\t\timpliedType := providerSchema.ResourceTypes[addr.Resource.Resource.Type].Block.ImpliedType()\n\t\t\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, providers.DeferredReasonResourceConfigUnknown, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:         addr,\n\t\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  addr,\n\t\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\t\t\tAction: plans.NoOp, // assume we'll get the config generation correct.\n\t\t\t\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(impliedType),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tAfter:  cty.UnknownVal(impliedType),\n\t\t\t\t\t\tImporting: &plans.Importing{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\tID: n.importTarget,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t})\n\t\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tvar readDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tinstanceRefreshState, readDiags = n.readResourceInstanceState(ctx, addr)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(readDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif deferred == nil {\n\t\t// We'll save a snapshot of what we just read from the state into the\n\t\t// prevRunState before we do anything else, since this will capture the\n\t\t// result of any schema upgrading that readResourceInstanceState just did,\n\t\t// but not include any out-of-band changes we might detect in in the\n\t\t// refresh step below.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, prevRunState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t\t// Also the refreshState, because that should still reflect schema upgrades\n\t\t// even if it doesn't reflect upstream changes.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// we may need to detect a change in CreateBeforeDestroy to ensure it's\n\t// stored when we are not refreshing\n\tupdatedCBD := false\n\tif n.Config != nil && n.Config.Managed != nil && instanceRefreshState != nil {\n\t\tnewCBD := n.Config.Managed.CreateBeforeDestroy || n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy\n\t\tupdatedCBD = instanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy != newCBD\n\t\tinstanceRefreshState.CreateBeforeDestroy = newCBD\n\t}\n\n\tvar refreshDeferred *providers.Deferred\n\t// This is the state of the resource before we refresh the value, we need to keep track\n\t// of this to report this as the before value if the refresh is deferred.\n\tpriorInstanceRefreshState := instanceRefreshState\n\n\t// Refresh, maybe\n\t// The import process handles its own refresh\n\tif !n.skipRefresh && !importing {\n\t\tvar refreshDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\t\tinstanceRefreshState, refreshDeferred, refreshDiags = n.refresh(ctx, states.NotDeposed, instanceRefreshState, ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed())\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif instanceRefreshState != nil {\n\t\t\t// When refreshing we start by merging the stored dependencies and\n\t\t\t// the configured dependencies. The configured dependencies will be\n\t\t\t// stored to state once the changes are applied. If the plan\n\t\t\t// results in no changes, we will re-write these dependencies\n\t\t\t// below.\n\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = mergeDeps(n.Dependencies, instanceRefreshState.Dependencies)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif deferred == nil && refreshDeferred != nil {\n\t\t\tdeferred = refreshDeferred\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif deferred == nil {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif n.skipRefresh && !importing && updatedCBD {\n\t\t// CreateBeforeDestroy must be set correctly in the state which is used\n\t\t// to create the apply graph, so if we did not refresh the state make\n\t\t// sure we still update any changes to CreateBeforeDestroy.\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// Plan the instance, unless we're in the refresh-only mode\n\tif !n.skipPlanChanges {\n\n\t\t// add this instance to n.forceReplace if replacement is triggered by\n\t\t// another change\n\t\trepData := instances.RepetitionData{}\n\t\tswitch k := addr.Resource.Key.(type) {\n\t\tcase addrs.IntKey:\n\t\t\trepData.CountIndex = k.Value()\n\t\tcase addrs.StringKey:\n\t\t\trepData.EachKey = k.Value()\n\t\t\trepData.EachValue = cty.DynamicVal\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.replaceTriggered(ctx, repData))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tchange, instancePlanState, planDeferred, repeatData, planDiags := n.plan(\n\t\t\tctx, nil, instanceRefreshState, n.ForceCreateBeforeDestroy, n.forceReplace,\n\t\t)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(planDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t// If we are importing and generating a configuration, we need to\n\t\t\t// ensure the change is written out so the configuration can be\n\t\t\t// captured.\n\t\t\tif planDeferred == nil && len(n.generateConfigPath) > 0 {\n\t\t\t\t// Update our return plan\n\t\t\t\tchange := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.prevRunAddr(ctx),\n\t\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\t\t\t// we only need a placeholder, so this will be a NoOp\n\t\t\t\t\t\tAction:          plans.NoOp,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tBefore:          instanceRefreshState.Value,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tAfter:           instanceRefreshState.Value,\n\t\t\t\t\t\tGeneratedConfig: n.generatedConfigHCL,\n\t\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif deferred == nil && planDeferred != nil {\n\t\t\tdeferred = planDeferred\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif importing {\n\t\t\tchange.Importing = &plans.Importing{ID: n.importTarget}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// FIXME: here we udpate the change to reflect the reason for\n\t\t// replacement, but we still overload forceReplace to get the correct\n\t\t// change planned.\n\t\tif len(n.replaceTriggeredBy) > 0 {\n\t\t\tchange.ActionReason = plans.ResourceInstanceReplaceByTriggers\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tdeferrals := ctx.Deferrals()\n\t\tif deferred != nil {\n\t\t\t// Then this resource has been deferred either during the import,\n\t\t\t// refresh or planning stage. We'll report the deferral and\n\t\t\t// store what we could produce in the deferral tracker.\n\t\t\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, change)\n\t\t} else if !deferrals.ShouldDeferResourceInstanceChanges(n.Addr, n.Dependencies) {\n\t\t\t// We intentionally write the change before the subsequent checks, because\n\t\t\t// all of the checks below this point are for problems caused by the\n\t\t\t// context surrounding the change, rather than the change itself, and\n\t\t\t// so it's helpful to still include the valid-in-isolation change as\n\t\t\t// part of the plan as additional context in our error output.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// FIXME: it is currently important that we write resource changes to\n\t\t\t// the plan (n.writeChange) before we write the corresponding state\n\t\t\t// (n.writeResourceInstanceState).\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// This is because the planned resource state will normally have the\n\t\t\t// status of states.ObjectPlanned, which causes later logic to refer to\n\t\t\t// the contents of the plan to retrieve the resource data. Because\n\t\t\t// there is no shared lock between these two data structures, reversing\n\t\t\t// the order of these writes will cause a brief window of inconsistency\n\t\t\t// which can lead to a failed safety check.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// Future work should adjust these APIs such that it is impossible to\n\t\t\t// update these two data structures incorrectly through any objects\n\t\t\t// reachable via the terraform.EvalContext API.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeChange(ctx, change, \"\"))\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instancePlanState, workingState))\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.checkPreventDestroy(change))\n\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// If this plan resulted in a NoOp, then apply won't have a chance to make\n\t\t\t// any changes to the stored dependencies. Since this is a NoOp we know\n\t\t\t// that the stored dependencies will have no effect during apply, and we can\n\t\t\t// write them out now.\n\t\t\tif change.Action == plans.NoOp && !depsEqual(instanceRefreshState.Dependencies, n.Dependencies) {\n\t\t\t\t// the refresh state will be the final state for this resource, so\n\t\t\t\t// finalize the dependencies here if they need to be updated.\n\t\t\t\tinstanceRefreshState.Dependencies = n.Dependencies\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, refreshState))\n\t\t\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t// Post-conditions might block completion. We intentionally do this\n\t\t\t// _after_ writing the state/diff because we want to check against\n\t\t\t// the result of the operation, and to fail on future operations\n\t\t\t// until the user makes the condition succeed.\n\t\t\t// (Note that some preconditions will end up being skipped during\n\t\t\t// planning, because their conditions depend on values not yet known.)\n\t\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\t\t\tctx, n.ResourceInstanceAddr(), repeatData,\n\t\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// The deferrals tracker says that we must defer changes for\n\t\t\t// this resource instance, presumably due to a dependency on an\n\t\t\t// upstream object that was already deferred. Therefore we just\n\t\t\t// report our own deferral (capturing a placeholder value in the\n\t\t\t// deferral tracker) and don't add anything to the plan or\n\t\t\t// working state.\n\t\t\t// In this case, the expression evaluator should use the placeholder\n\t\t\t// value registered here as the value of this resource instance,\n\t\t\t// instead of using the plan.\n\t\t\tdeferrals.ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(n.Addr, providers.DeferredReasonDeferredPrereq, change)\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// In refresh-only mode we need to evaluate the for-each expression in\n\t\t// order to supply the value to the pre- and post-condition check\n\t\t// blocks. This has the unfortunate edge case of a refresh-only plan\n\t\t// executing with a for-each map which has the same keys but different\n\t\t// values, which could result in a post-condition check relying on that\n\t\t// value being inaccurate. Unless we decide to store the value of the\n\t\t// for-each expression in state, this is unavoidable.\n\t\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n\t\trepeatData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n\n\t\tcheckDiags := evalCheckRules(\n\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePrecondition,\n\t\t\tn.Config.Preconditions,\n\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n\t\t)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\n\t\t// Even if we don't plan changes, we do still need to at least update\n\t\t// the working state to reflect the refresh result. If not, then e.g.\n\t\t// any output values refering to this will not react to the drift.\n\t\t// (Even if we didn't actually refresh above, this will still save\n\t\t// the result of any schema upgrading we did in readResourceInstanceState.)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(n.writeResourceInstanceState(ctx, instanceRefreshState, workingState))\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\treturn diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Here we also evaluate post-conditions after updating the working\n\t\t// state, because we want to check against the result of the refresh.\n\t\t// Unlike in normal planning mode, these checks are still evaluated\n\t\t// even if pre-conditions generated diagnostics, because we have no\n\t\t// planned changes to block.\n\t\tcheckDiags = evalCheckRules(\n\t\t\taddrs.ResourcePostcondition,\n\t\t\tn.Config.Postconditions,\n\t\t\tctx, addr, repeatData,\n\t\t\tcheckRuleSeverity,\n\t\t)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(checkDiags)\n\n\t\t// In this case we skipped planning changes and therefore need to report the deferral\n\t\t// here, if there was one.\n\t\tif refreshDeferred != nil {\n\t\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceInstanceDeferred(addr, deferred.Reason, &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\t\t\tAddr:         n.Addr,\n\t\t\t\tPrevRunAddr:  n.Addr,\n\t\t\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider,\n\t\t\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n\t\t\t\t\tBefore: priorInstanceRefreshState.Value,\n\t\t\t\t\tAfter:  instanceRefreshState.Value,\n\t\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t})\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// replaceTriggered checks if this instance needs to be replace due to a change\n// in a replace_triggered_by reference. If replacement is required, the\n// instance address is added to forceReplace\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) replaceTriggered(ctx EvalContext, repData instances.RepetitionData) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\treturn diags\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, expr := range n.Config.TriggersReplacement {\n\t\tref, replace, evalDiags := ctx.EvaluateReplaceTriggeredBy(expr, repData)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(evalDiags)\n\t\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif replace {\n\t\t\t// FIXME: forceReplace accomplishes the same goal, however we may\n\t\t\t// want to communicate more information about which resource\n\t\t\t// triggered the replacement in the plan.\n\t\t\t// Rather than further complicating the plan method with more\n\t\t\t// options, we can refactor both of these features later.\n\t\t\tn.forceReplace = append(n.forceReplace, n.Addr)\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[DEBUG] ReplaceTriggeredBy forcing replacement of %s due to change in %s\", n.Addr, ref.DisplayString())\n\n\t\t\tn.replaceTriggeredBy = append(n.replaceTriggeredBy, ref)\n\t\t\tbreak\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn diags\n}\n\nfunc (n *NodePlannableResourceInstance) importState(ctx EvalContext, addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance, importId string, provider providers.Interface, providerSchema providers.ProviderSchema) (*states.ResourceInstanceObject, *providers.Deferred, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tdeferralAllowed := ctx.Deferrals().DeferralAllowed()\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tabsAddr := addr.Resource.Absolute(ctx.Path())\n\thookResourceID := HookResourceIdentity{\n\t\tAddr:         absAddr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.ResolvedProvider.Provider,\n\t}\n\n\tvar deferred *providers.Deferred\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(ctx.Hook(func(h Hook) (HookAction, error) {\n\t\treturn h.PrePlanImport(hookResourceID, importId)\n\t}))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.Addr.Resource.Resource)\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type for %q\", n.Addr))\n\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n\t}\n\n\tvar resp providers.ImportResourceStateResponse\n\tif n.override != nil {\n\t\t// For overriding resources that are being imported, we cheat a little\n\t\t// bit and look ahead at the configuration the user has provided and\n\t\t// we'll use that as the basis for the resource we're going to make up\n\t\t// that is due to be overridden.\n\n\t\t// Note, we know we have configuration as it's impossible to enable\n\t\t// config generation during tests, and the validation that config exists\n\t\t// if configuration generation is off has already happened.\n\t\tif n.Config == nil {\n\t\t\t// But, just in case we change this at some point in the future,\n\t\t\t// let's add a specific error message here we can test for to\n\t\t\t// document the expectation somewhere. This shouldn't happen in\n\t\t\t// production, so we don't bother with a pretty error.\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"override blocks do not support config generation\"))\n\t\t\treturn nil, deferred, diags\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tforEach, _, _ := evaluateForEachExpression(n.Config.ForEach, ctx, false)\n\t\tkeyData := EvalDataForInstanceKey(n.ResourceInstanceAddr().Resource.Key, forEach)\n\t\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.Config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n\t\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\t\t// We have an overridden resource so we're definitely in a test and\n\t\t\t// the users config is not valid. So give up and just report the\n\t\t\t// problems in the users configuration. Normally, we'd import the\n\t\t\t// resource before giving up but for a test it doesn't matter, the\n\t\t\t// test fails in the same way and the state is just lost anyway.\n\t\t\t//\n\t\t\t// If there returns the union of 2 sets of dependencies\nfunc mergeDeps(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) []addrs.ConfigResource {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase len(a) == 0:\n\t\treturn b\n\tcase len(b) == 0:\n\t\treturn a\n\t}\n\n\tset := make(map[string]addrs.ConfigResource)\n\n\tfor _, dep := range a {\n\t\tset[dep.String()] = dep\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, dep := range b {\n\t\tset[dep.String()] = dep\n\t}\n\n\tnewDeps := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, 0, len(set))\n\tfor _, dep := range set {\n\t\tnewDeps = append(newDeps, dep)\n\t}\n\n\treturn newDeps\n}\n\nfunc depsEqual(a, b []addrs.ConfigResource) bool {\n\tif len(a) != len(b) {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\t// Because we need to sort the deps to compare equality, make shallow\n\t// copies to prevent concurrently modifying the array values on\n\t// dependencies shared between expanded instances.\n\tcopyA, copyB := make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(a)), make([]addrs.ConfigResource, len(b))\n\tcopy(copyA, a)\n\tcopy(copyB, b)\n\ta, b = copyA, copyB\n\n\tless := func(s []addrs.ConfigResource) func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\treturn func(i, j int) bool {\n\t\t\treturn s[i].String() < s[j].String()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tsort.Slice(a, less(a))\n\tsort.Slice(b, less(b))\n\n\tfor i := range a {\n\t\tif !a[i].Equal(b[i]) {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn true\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":30,"oldLines":7,"newStart":30,"newLines":7,"lines":[" // instance that is plannable. It doesn't make sense to use this\n\t// transformer without setting at least one of these, since that would\n\t// skip everything and thus be a no-op.\n\tConcreteCurrent ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tConcreteDeposed ConcreteResourceInstanceDeposedNodeFunc\n\n\tState *states.State\n}\n\nfunc (t *StateTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif t.State == nil {\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: state is nil, so nothing to do\")\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil && t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for both current and deposed instance objects\")\n\tcase t.ConcreteCurrent != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for current instance objects only\")\n\tcase t.ConcreteDeposed != nil:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: creating nodes for deposed instance objects only\")\n\tdefault:\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: pointless no-op call, creating no nodes at all\")\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, ms := range t.State.Modules {\n\t\tfor _, rs := range ms.Resources {\n\t\t\tresourceAddr := rs.Addr\n\n\t\t\tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n\t\t\t\taddr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)\n\n\t\t\t\tif obj := is.Current; obj != nil && t.ConcreteCurrent != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteCurrent(abstract)\n\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s current object\", node, addr)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\tif t.ConcreteDeposed != nil {\n\t\t\t\t\tfor dk := range is.Deposed {\n\t\t\t\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tnode := t.ConcreteDeposed(abstract, dk)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] StateTransformer: added %T for %s deposed object %s\", node, addr, dk)\n\t\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn nil\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":53,"oldLines":7,"newStart":53,"newLines":7,"lines":["         addr := resourceAddr.Instance(key)"," ","         if obj := is.Current; {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_01KzPG7qEMZsQWM3CgTEp34m","type":"tool_result","content":"The file /workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go has been updated. All occurrences of 'NodeAbstractResourceInstance' were successfully replaced with 'NodeResourceInstanceBase'."}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"463fa0db-166c-4382-a0fc-587c21bbce2a","uuid":"08a5da7b-7fc6-4a03-9f88-4872fa0c1eab","tool_use_result":{"filePath":"/workspace/internal/terraform/transform_diff.go","oldString":"NodeAbstractResourceInstance","newString":"NodeResourceInstanceBase","originalFile":"// Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.\n// SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1\n\npackage terraform\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/dag\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// DiffTransformer is a GraphTransformer that adds graph nodes representing\n// each of the resource changes described in the given Changes object.\ntype DiffTransformer struct {\n\tConcrete ConcreteResourceInstanceNodeFunc\n\tState    *states.State\n\tChanges  *plans.ChangesSrc\n\tConfig   *configs.Config\n}\n\n// return true if the given resource instance has either Preconditions or\n// Postconditions defined in the configuration.\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) hasConfigConditions(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) bool {\n\t// unit tests may have no config\n\tif t.Config == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tcfg := t.Config.DescendantForInstance(addr.Module)\n\tif cfg == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tres := cfg.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr.ConfigResource().Resource)\n\tif res == nil {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\treturn len(res.Preconditions) > 0 || len(res.Postconditions) > 0\n}\n\nfunc (t *DiffTransformer) Transform(g *Graph) error {\n\tif t.Changes == nil || len(t.Changes.Resources) == 0 {\n\t\t// Nothing to do!\n\t\treturn nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Go through all the modules in the diff.\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer starting\")\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tstate := t.State\n\tchanges := t.Changes\n\n\t// DiffTransformer creates resource _instance_ nodes. If there are any\n\t// whole-resource nodes already in the graph, we must ensure that they\n\t// get evaluated before any of the corresponding instances by creating\n\t// dependency edges, so we'll do some prep work here to ensure we'll only\n\t// create connections to nodes that existed before we started here.\n\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, rc := range changes.Resources {\n\t\taddr := rc.Addr\n\t\tdk := rc.DeposedKey\n\n\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: found %s change for %s %s\", rc.Action, addr, dk)\n\n\t\t// Depending on the action we'll need some different combinations of\n\t\t// nodes, because destroying uses a special node type separate from\n\t\t// other actions.\n\t\tvar update, delete, forget, createBeforeDestroy bool\n\t\tswitch rc.Action {\n\t\tcase plans.NoOp:\n\t\t\t// For a no-op change we don't take any action but we still\n\t\t\t// run any condition checks associated with the object, to\n\t\t\t// make sure that they still hold when considering the\n\t\t\t// results of other changes.\n\t\t\tupdate = t.hasConfigConditions(addr)\n\t\tcase plans.Delete:\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\tcase plans.DeleteThenCreate, plans.CreateThenDelete:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t\tdelete = true\n\t\t\tcreateBeforeDestroy = (rc.Action == plans.CreateThenDelete)\n\t\tcase plans.Forget:\n\t\t\tforget = true\n\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\tupdate = true\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// A deposed instance may only have a change of Delete, Forget, or NoOp.\n\t\t// A NoOp can happen if the provider shows it no longer exists during\n\t\t// the most recent ReadResource operation.\n\t\tif dk != states.NotDeposed && !(rc.Action == plans.Delete || rc.Action == plans.Forget || rc.Action == plans.NoOp) {\n\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change for deposed object\",\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a non-remove change for %s deposed object %s. The only valid actions for a deposed object are to destroy it or remove it from state, so this is a bug in Terraform.\", addr, dk),\n\t\t\t))\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// If we're going to do a create_before_destroy Replace operation then\n\t\t// we need to allocate a DeposedKey to use to retain the\n\t\t// not-yet-destroyed prior object, so that the delete node can destroy\n\t\t// _that_ rather than the newly-created node, which will be current\n\t\t// by the time the delete node is visited.\n\t\tif update && delete && createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t// In this case, variable dk will be the _pre-assigned_ DeposedKey\n\t\t\t// that must be used if the update graph node deposes the current\n\t\t\t// instance, which will then align with the same key we pass\n\t\t\t// into the destroy node to ensure we destroy exactly the deposed\n\t\t\t// object we expect.\n\t\t\tif state != nil {\n\t\t\t\tris := state.ResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\t\tif ris == nil {\n\t\t\t\t\t// Should never happen, since we don't plan to replace an\n\t\t\t\t\t// instance that doesn't exist yet.\n\t\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Invalid planned change\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"The plan contains a replace change for %s, which doesn't exist yet. This is a bug in Terraform.\", addr),\n\t\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\t\t// Allocating a deposed key separately from using it can be racy\n\t\t\t\t// in general, but we assume here that nothing except the apply\n\t\t\t\t// node we instantiate below will actually make new deposed objects\n\t\t\t\t// in practice, and so the set of already-used keys will not change\n\t\t\t\t// between now and then.\n\t\t\t\tdk = ris.FindUnusedDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\t// If we have no state at all yet then we can use _any_\n\t\t\t\t// DeposedKey.\n\t\t\t\tdk = states.NewDeposedKey()\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif update {\n\t\t\t// All actions except destroying the node type chosen by t.Concrete\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tvar node dag.Vertex = abstract\n\t\t\tif f := t.Concrete; f != nil {\n\t\t\t\tnode = f(abstract)\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tif createBeforeDestroy {\n\t\t\t\t// We'll attach our pre-allocated DeposedKey to the node if\n\t\t\t\t// it supports that. NodeApplyableResourceInstance is the\n\t\t\t\t// specific concrete node type we are looking for here really,\n\t\t\t\t// since that's the only node type that might depose objects.\n\t\t\t\tif dn, ok := node.(GraphNodeDeposer); ok {\n\t\t\t\t\tdn.SetPreallocatedDeposedKey(dk)\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s, deposing prior object to %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node), dk)\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t\tfor _, rsrcNode := range resourceNodes.Get(addr.ConfigResource()) {\n\t\t\t\tg.Connect(dag.BasicEdge(node, rsrcNode))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif delete {\n\t\t\t// Destroying always uses a destroy-specific node type, though\n\t\t\t// which one depends on whether we're destroying a current object\n\t\t\t// or a deposed object.\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeDestroyDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s deposed object %s will be represented for destruction by %s\", addr, dk, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t\tif forget {\n\t\t\tvar node GraphNodeResourceInstance\n\t\t\tabstract := NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(addr)\n\t\t\tif dk == states.NotDeposed {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetResourceInstance{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t\tnode = &NodeForgetDeposedResourceInstanceObject{\n\t\t\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: abstract,\n\t\t\t\t\tDeposedKey:                   dk,\n\t\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t}\n\n\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer: %s will be represented for forgetting by %s\", addr, dag.VertexName(node))\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\t\t}\n\n\t}\n\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] DiffTransformer complete\")\n\n\treturn diags.Err()\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":160,"oldLines":7,"newStart":160,"newLines":7,"lines":[" ","     if update {","       // All actions except destroying the node type chosen by test case covering the very narrow situation\n\t// where a root module output value depends on a resource, where we\n\t// need to make sure that the output value doesn't block pruning of\n\t// the resource from the graph. This special case exists because although\n\t// root module objects are \"expanders\", they in practice always expand\n\t// to exactly one instance and so don't have the usual requirement of\n\t// needing to stick around in order to support downstream expanders\n\t// when there are e.g. nested expanding modules.\n\n\t// In order to keep this test focused on the pruneUnusedNodesTransformer\n\t// as much as possible we're using a minimal graph construction here which\n\t// is just enough to get the nodes we need, but this does mean that this\n\t// test might be invalidated by future changes to the apply graph builder,\n\t// and so if something seems off here it might help to compare the\n\t// following with the real apply graph transformer and verify whether\n\t// this smaller construction is still realistic enough to be a valid test.\n\t// It might be valid to change or remove this test to \"make it work\", as\n\t// long as you verify that there is still _something_ upholding the\n\t// invariant that a root module output value should not block a resource\n\t// node from being pruned from the graph.\n\n\tconcreteResource := func(a *NodeAbstractResource) dag.Vertex {\n\t\treturn resourceInstAddr,\n\t\tPrevRunAddr:  resourceInstAddr,\n\t\tProviderAddr: providerCfgAddr,\n\t\tChangeSrc: plans.ChangeSrc{\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Delete,\n\t\t\tBefore: emptyObjDynamicVal,\n\t\t\tAfter:  nullObjDynamicVal,\n\t\t},\n\t})\n\n\tbuilder := &BasicGraphBuilder{\n\t\tSteps: []GraphTransformer{\n\t\t\t&ConfigTransformer{\n\t\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResource,\n\t\t\t\tConfig:   config,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t&OutputTransformer{\n\t\t\t\tConfig: config,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t&DiffTransformer{\n\t\t\t\tConcrete: concreteResourceInstance,\n\t\t\t\tState:    state,\n\t\t\t\tChanges:  changes,\n\t\t\t},\n\t\t\t&ReferenceTransformer{},\n\t\t\t&AttachDependenciesTransformer{},\n\t\t\t&pruneUnusedNodesTransformer{},\n\t\t\t&CloseRootModuleTransformer{},\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\tgraph, diags := \"test.a (expand)\" node\n\t// should've been pruned in recognition of the fact that we're performing\n\t// a destroy and therefore we only need the \"test.a (destroy)\" node.\n\n\tnodesByName := (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"log\"\n\t\"strings\"\n\n\t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/marks\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans/objchange\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers\"\n\t\"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags\"\n)\n\n// nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource is a graph node that stands in for\n// an unbounded set of potential resource instances that we don't yet know.\n//\n// Its job is to check the configuration as much as we can with the information\n// that's available (so we can raise an error early if something is clearly\n// wrong across _all_ potential instances) and to record a placeholder value\n// for use when evaluating other objects that refer to this resource.\n//\n// This is the partial-expanded equivalent of NodePlannableResourceInstance.\ntype nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource struct {\n\taddr             addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n\tconfig           *configs.Resource\n\tresolvedProvider addrs.AbsProviderConfig\n\tskipPlanChanges  bool\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ graphNodeEvalContextScope = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeConfigResource   = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n\t_ GraphNodeExecutable       = (*nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource)(nil)\n)\n\n// Name implements [dag.NamedVertex].\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Name() string {\n\treturn n.addr.String()\n}\n\n// Path implements graphNodeEvalContextScope.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Path() evalContextScope {\n\tif moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.ModuleInstance(); ok {\n\t\treturn evalContextModuleInstance{Addr: moduleAddr}\n\t} else if moduleAddr, ok := n.addr.PartialExpandedModule(); ok {\n\t\treturn evalContextPartialExpandedModule{Addr: moduleAddr}\n\t} else {\n\t\t// Should not get here: at least one of the two cases above\n\t\t// should always be true for any valid addrs.PartialExpandedResource\n\t\tpanic(\"addrs.PartialExpandedResource has neither a partial-expanded or a fully-expanded module instance address\")\n\t}\n}\n\n// ResourceAddr implements GraphNodeConfigResource.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) ResourceAddr() addrs.ConfigResource {\n\treturn n.addr.ConfigResource()\n}\n\n// Execute implements GraphNodeExecutable.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) Execute(ctx EvalContext, op walkOperation) tfdiags.Diagnostics {\n\t// Because this node type implements [graphNodeEvalContextScope], the\n\t// given EvalContext could either be for a fully-expanded module instance\n\t// or an unbounded set of potential module instances sharing a common\n\t// prefix. The logic here doesn't need to vary between the two because\n\t// the differences are encapsulated in the EvalContext abstraction,\n\t// but if you're unsure which of the two is being used then look for\n\t// the following line in the logs to see if there's a [*] marker on\n\t// any of the module instance steps, or if the [*] is applied only to\n\t// the resource itself.\n\t//\n\t// Fully-expanded module example:\n\t//\n\t//     module.foo[\"a\"].type.name[*]\n\t//\n\t// Partial-expanded module example:\n\t//\n\t//     module.foo[*].type.name[*]\n\t//\n\tlog.Printf(\"[TRACE] nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource: checking all of %s\", n.addr.String())\n\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\tswitch n.addr.Resource().Mode {\n\tcase addrs.ManagedResourceMode:\n\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.managedResourceExecute(ctx)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportResourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n\tcase addrs.DataResourceMode:\n\t\tchange, changeDiags := n.dataResourceExecute(ctx)\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(changeDiags)\n\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportDataSourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr, change)\n\tcase addrs.EphemeralResourceMode:\n\t\tctx.Deferrals().ReportEphemeralResourceExpansionDeferred(n.addr)\n\tdefault:\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported resource mode %s\", n.config.Mode))\n\t}\n\n\t// Registering this allows downstream resources that depend on this one\n\t// to know that they need to defer themselves too, in order to preserve\n\t// correct dependency order.\n\treturn diags\n}\n\n// Logic here mirrors (*NodePlannableResourceInstance).managedResourceExecute.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) managedResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// We cannot fully plan partial-expanded resources because we don't know\n\t// what addresses they will have, but in this function we'll still go\n\t// through many of the familiar motions of planning so that we can give\n\t// feedback sooner if we can prove that the configuration is already\n\t// invalid even with the partial information we have here. This is just\n\t// to shorten the iterative journey, so nothing here actually contributes\n\t// new actions to the plan.\n\n\t// We'll make a basic change for us to use as a placeholder for the time\n\t// being, and we'll populate it as we get more info.\n\tchange := plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:         n.addr.UnknownResourceInstance(),\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.resolvedProvider,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t// We don't actually know the action, but we simulate the plan later\n\t\t\t// as a create action so we'll use that here too.\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Create,\n\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\tAfter:  cty.DynamicVal, // This will be populated later\n\t\t},\n\t}\n\n\tprovider, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.resolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(n.addr.Resource(), n.config.Config, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.addr.Resource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", n.addr.Resource().Type))\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: Normal managed resource planning\n\t// (in [NodePlannableResourceInstance.managedResourceExecute]) deals with\n\t// some additional things that we're just ignoring here for now. We should\n\t// confirm whether it's really okay to ignore them here or if we ought\n\t// to be partial-populating some results.\n\t//\n\t// Including but not necessarily limited to:\n\t// - Somehow reacting if one or more of the possible resource instances\n\t//   is affected by an import block\n\t// - Evaluating the preconditions/postconditions to see if they produce\n\t//   a definitive fail result even with the partial information.\n\n\tif n.skipPlanChanges {\n\t\t// If we're supposed to be making a refresh-only plan then there's\n\t\t// not really anything else to do here, since we can only refresh\n\t\t// specific known resource instances (which another graph node should\n\t\t// handle), so we'll just return early.\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tkeyData := n.keyData()\n\n\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedConfigVal, _ := configVal.UnmarkDeep()\n\tvalidateResp := provider.ValidateResourceConfig(\n\t\tproviders.ValidateResourceConfigRequest{\n\t\t\tTypeName: n.addr.Resource().Type,\n\t\t\tConfig:   unmarkedConfigVal,\n\t\t},\n\t)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(validateResp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.config.Config, n.addr.String()))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\tpriorVal := cty.NullVal(schema.ImpliedType()) // we don't have any specific prior value to use\n\tproposedNewVal := objchange.ProposedNew(schema, priorVal, unmarkedConfigVal)\n\n\t// The provider now gets to plan an imaginary substitute that represents\n\t// all of the possible resource instances together. Correctly-implemented\n\t// providers should handle the extra unknown values here just as if they\n\t// had been unknown an individual instance's configuration, but we can\n\t// still find out if any of the known values are somehow invalid and\n\t// learn a subset of the \"computed\" attribute values to save as part\n\t// of our placeholder value for downstream checks.\n\tresp := provider.PlanResourceChange(providers.PlanResourceChangeRequest{\n\t\tTypeName:         n.addr.Resource().Type,\n\t\tConfig:           unmarkedConfigVal,\n\t\tPriorState:       priorVal,\n\t\tProposedNewState: proposedNewVal,\n\t\t// TODO: Should we send \"ProviderMeta\" here? We don't have the\n\t\t// necessary data for that wired through here right now, but\n\t\t// we might need to do that before stabilizing support for unknown\n\t\t// resource instance expansion.\n\t})\n\tdiags = diags.Append(resp.Diagnostics.InConfigBody(n.config.Config, n.addr.String()))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tplannedNewVal := resp.PlannedState\n\tif plannedNewVal == cty.NilVal {\n\t\t// Should never happen. Since real-world providers return via RPC a nil\n\t\t// is always a bug in the client-side stub. This is more likely caused\n\t\t// by an incompletely-configured mock provider in tests, though.\n\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"PlanResourceChange of %s produced nil value\", n.addr.String()))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, err := range plannedNewVal.Type().TestConformance(schema.ImpliedType()) {\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\tn.resolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.addr.String()),\n\t\t\t),\n\t\t))\n\t}\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tif errs := objchange.AssertPlanValid(schema, priorVal, unmarkedConfigVal, plannedNewVal); len(errs) > 0 {\n\t\tif resp.LegacyTypeSystem {\n\t\t\t// The shimming of the old type system in the legacy SDK is not precise\n\t\t\t// enough to pass this consistency check, so we'll give it a pass here,\n\t\t\t// but we will generate a warning about it so that we are more likely\n\t\t\t// to notice in the logs if an inconsistency beyond the type system\n\t\t\t// leads to a downstream provider failure.\n\t\t\tvar buf strings.Builder\n\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf,\n\t\t\t\t\"[WARN] Provider %q produced an invalid plan for %s, but we are tolerating it because it is using the legacy plugin SDK.\\n    The following problems may be the cause of any confusing errors from downstream operations:\",\n\t\t\t\tn.resolvedProvider.Provider, n.addr.String(),\n\t\t\t)\n\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\tfmt.Fprintf(&buf, \"\\n      - %s\", tfdiags.FormatError(err))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tlog.Print(buf.String())\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tfor _, err := range errs {\n\t\t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(\n\t\t\t\t\ttfdiags.Error,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider produced invalid plan\",\n\t\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\n\t\t\t\t\t\t\"Provider %q planned an invalid value for %s.\\n\\nThis is a bug in the provider, which should be reported in the provider's own issue tracker.\",\n\t\t\t\t\t\tn.resolvedProvider.Provider, tfdiags.FormatErrorPrefixed(err, n.addr.String()),\n\t\t\t\t\t),\n\t\t\t\t))\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\t// We need to combine the dynamic marks with the static marks implied by\n\t// the provider's schema.\n\tplannedNewVal = plannedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(plannedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n\t\tplannedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(plannedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n\t}\n\n\tchange.After = plannedNewVal\n\tchange.Private = resp.PlannedPrivate\n\treturn &change, diags\n}\n\n// Logic here mirrors a combination of (*NodePlannableResourceInstance).dataResourceExecute\n// and (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance).planDataSource.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n\n\t// Start with a basic change, then attempt to fill in the After value.\n\tchange := plans.ResourceInstanceChange{\n\t\tAddr:         n.addr.UnknownResourceInstance(),\n\t\tProviderAddr: n.resolvedProvider,\n\t\tChange: plans.Change{\n\t\t\t// Data sources can only Read.\n\t\t\tAction: plans.Read,\n\t\t\tBefore: cty.NullVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType),\n\t\t\tAfter:  cty.DynamicVal, // hoping to fill this in\n\t\t},\n\t\t// For now, this is the default reason for deferred data source reads.\n\t\t// It's _basically_ the truth!\n\t\tActionReason: plans.ResourceInstanceReadBecauseConfigUnknown,\n\t}\n\n\t// Unlike with the managed path, we don't ask the provider to *do* anything.\n\t_, providerSchema, err := getProvider(ctx, n.resolvedProvider)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(err)\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tdiags = diags.Append(validateSelfRef(n.addr.Resource(), n.config.Config, providerSchema))\n\tif diags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// This is the point where we switch to mirroring logic from\n\t// NodeAbstractResourceInstance's planDataSource. If you were curious.\n\n\tschema, _ := providerSchema.SchemaForResourceAddr(n.addr.Resource())\n\tif schema == nil {\n\t\t// Should be caught during validation, so we don't bother with a pretty error here\n\t\tdiags = diags.Append(fmt.Errorf(\"provider does not support resource type %q\", n.addr.Resource().Type))\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\tkeyData := n.keyData()\n\n\tconfigVal, _, configDiags := ctx.EvaluateBlock(n.config.Config, schema, nil, keyData)\n\tdiags = diags.Append(configDiags)\n\tif configDiags.HasErrors() {\n\t\treturn &change, diags\n\t}\n\n\t// Note: We're deliberately not doing anything special for nested-in-a-check\n\t// data sources. (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance).planDataSource has some\n\t// special handling for these, but it's founded on the assumption that we're\n\t// going to be able to actually read the data source. (Specifically: it\n\t// blocks propagation of errors on read during plan, and ensures that we get\n\t// a planned Read to execute during apply even if the data source would have\n\t// been readable earlier.) But we're getting deferred anyway, so none of\n\t// that is relevant on this path. 👍🏼\n\n\t// Unlike the managed path, we don't call provider.ValidateResourceConfig;\n\t// Terraform handles planning for data sources without hands-on input from\n\t// the provider. BTW, this is about where we start mirroring planDataSource's\n\t// logic for a data source with unknown config, which is sort of what we\n\t// are, after all.\n\tunmarkedConfigVal, unmarkedPaths := configVal.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()\n\tproposedNewVal := objchange.PlannedDataResourceObject(schema, unmarkedConfigVal)\n\tproposedNewVal = proposedNewVal.MarkWithPaths(unmarkedPaths)\n\tif sensitivePaths := schema.SensitivePaths(proposedNewVal, nil); len(sensitivePaths) != 0 {\n\t\tproposedNewVal = marks.MarkPaths(proposedNewVal, marks.Sensitive, sensitivePaths)\n\t}\n\t// yay we made it\n\tchange.After = proposedNewVal\n\treturn &change, diags\n}\n\n// keyData returns suitable unknown values for count.index, each.key, and\n// each.value, based on what we know of the resource config. When evaluating\n// with this unknown key data, anything that varies between the instances will\n// be unknown but we can still check the arguments that they all have in common.\nfunc (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) keyData() instances.RepetitionData {\n\tswitch {\n\tcase n.config.ForEach != nil:\n\t\t// We don't actually know the `for_each` type here, but we do at least\n\t\t// know it's for_each.\n\t\treturn instances.UnknownForEachRepetitionData(cty.DynamicPseudoType)\n\tcase n.config.Count != nil:\n\t\treturn instances.UnknownCountRepetitionData\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// If we get here then we're presumably a single-instance resource\n\t\t// inside a multi-instance module whose instances aren't known yet,\n\t\t// and so we'll evaluate without any of the repetition symbols to\n\t\t// still generate the usual errors if someone tries to use them here.\n\t\treturn instances.RepetitionData{\n\t\t\tCountIndex: cty.NilVal,\n\t\t\tEachKey:    cty.NilVal,\n\t\t\tEachValue:  cty.NilVal,\n\t\t}\n\t}\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":285,"oldLines":7,"newStart":285,"newLines":7,"lines":[" }"," "," // Logic here mirrors a combination of (*NodePlannableResourceInstance).dataResourceExecute","-// and (*NodeAbstractResourceInstance).planDataSource.","+// and (*NodeResourceInstanceBase).planDataSource."," func (n *nodePlannablePartialExpandedResource) dataResourceExecute(ctx EvalContext) (*plans.ResourceInstanceChange, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {","   var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics"," "]},{"oldStart":317,"oldLines":7,"newStart":317,"newLines":7,"lines":["   }"," ","   // This is the point where we switch to mirroring logic from","-  // NodeAbstractResourceInstance's planDataSource. If there are any whole-resource nodes already in the\n\t// graph, we must ensure they get evaluated before any of the corresponding\n\t// instances by creating dependency edges.\n\tresourceNodes := addrs.MakeMap[addrs.ConfigResource, []GraphNodeConfigResource]()\n\tfor _, node := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\trn, ok := node.(GraphNodeConfigResource)\n\t\tif !ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\t\t// We ignore any instances that _also_ implement\n\t\t// GraphNodeResourceInstance, since in the unlikely event that they\n\t\t// do exist we'd probably end up creating cycles by connecting them.\n\t\tif _, ok := node.(GraphNodeResourceInstance); ok {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\trAddr := rn.ResourceAddr()\n\t\tresourceNodes.Put(rAddr, append(resourceNodes.Get(rAddr), rn))\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, change := range t.DeferredChanges {\n\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredInstance{\n\t\t\tNodeAbstractResourceInstance: NewNodeAbstractResourceInstance(change.ChangeSrc.Addr),\n\t\t\tReason:                       change.DeferredReason,\n\t\t\tChangeSrc:                    change.ChangeSrc,\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Create a special node for partial instances, that handles the\n\t\t// addresses a little differently.\n\t\tif change.DeferredReason == providers.DeferredReasonInstanceCountUnknown {\n\t\t\tper := change.ChangeSrc.Addr.PartialResource()\n\n\t\t\t// This is a partial instance, so we need to create a partial node\n\t\t\t// instead of a full instance node.\n\t\t\tnode := &nodeApplyableDeferredPartialInstance{\n\t\t\t\tnodeApplyableDeferredInstance: node,\n\t\t\t\tPartialAddr:                   per,\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tg.Add(node)\n\n\t\t\t// Now we want to find the expansion node that would be applied for\n\t\t\t// this resource, and tell it that it is performing a partial\n\t\t\t// expansion.\n\t\t\tfor _, v := range g.Vertices() {\n\t\t\t\tif n, ok := v.(*nodeExpandApplyableResource); {\n\t*NodeAbstractResourceInstance\n\n\t// skipRefresh indicates that we should skip refreshing individual instances\n\tskipRefresh bool\n\n\t// skipPlanChanges indicates we should skip trying to plan change actions\n\t// for any instances.\n\tskipPlanChanges bool\n\n\t// forgetResources lists resources that should not be destroyed, only removed\n\t// from state.\n\tforgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource\n\n\t// forgetModules lists modules that should not be destroyed, only removed\n\t// from state.\n\tforgetModules []addrs.Module\n}\n\nvar (\n\t_ GraphNodeModuleInstance       =
